findings.title: .ts extension in ESM import paths causes ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND
findings.detail: src/orchestrator/approval-gate.ts lines 19-20 import './goal-proposals-store.ts' and '../agents/proposer/types.ts' with .ts extensions. Node ESM module resolution does not rewrite .ts extensions — at runtime these resolve to the literal .ts path which doesn't exist as a built artifact. Must be .js per the TypeScript ESM convention.
findings.fix_sketch: Change both import extensions from .ts to .js

findings.title: rowCount === 1 without null-guard — pg driver can return null
findings.detail: src/orchestrator/goal-proposals-store.ts lines 120, 180, 210 compare result.rowCount === 1. The pg driver types rowCount as number | null — it returns null for non-DML statements and on some edge cases. Without ?? 0 the comparison silently fails, returning { updated: false } when the row actually was updated.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace result.rowCount === 1 with (result.rowCount ?? 0) === 1 at all three sites

findings.title: postDiscordAlert swallows HTTP 4xx/5xx responses silently
findings.detail: src/orchestrator/index.ts postDiscordAlert (lines 496-509) calls res.resume() and resolves on 'end' without checking res.statusCode. A 429 rate-limit or 400 bad-request from Discord resolves the promise as success. Operators will never see Discord delivery failures.
findings.fix_sketch: In the response handler, check res.statusCode >= 400 and log a warning before resolving

findings.title: Discord send() called before insertProposal — orphan messages on DB failure
findings.detail: src/discord/proposals.ts lines 171-190: channel.send() fires first, then insertProposal(). If the Discord post succeeds but the Postgres insert throws, the Discord message with Approve/Reject buttons exists with no backing DB row. Clicking the buttons will fail with 'no matching proposal' and the orphaned buttons can never be actioned.
findings.fix_sketch: Swap order: call insertProposal() first, then channel.send(). On send failure the DB row can be GC'd by a future sweep.

findings.title: sprint-bridge sleep is unconditional — delays task pickup by tickMs per task
findings.detail: src/orchestrator/handlers/sprint-bridge.ts line 126: await sleep(tickMs) fires unconditionally at the bottom of the poll loop, even when a task was just picked up. Under load with N queued tasks, each task is delayed by tickMs (typically 5s) giving O(N*5s) total drain time instead of O(5s).
findings.fix_sketch: Wrap the sleep in if (!task) so it only fires when the queue was empty

findings.title: listActiveSprints fetches all sprint rows with no LIMIT — unbounded scan
findings.detail: dashboard/server.ts lines 82-113: SELECT ... FROM sprints ORDER BY updated_at DESC with no WHERE or LIMIT. On a long-running system this becomes a full table scan on every poll interval. Should cap at a reasonable recent window.
findings.fix_sketch: Add LIMIT 200 to the sprints query (active sprints are always a small subset of recent rows)

findings.title: execSync inside async function blocks Node event loop during Supabase sync
findings.detail: scripts/audit-ritual.ts line 155: execSync('npx tsx scripts/sync-audit-findings.ts ...') is called inside an async reconcile function. execSync blocks the event loop for the duration of the child process, starving any pending I/O (timers, network callbacks). execFileAsync is already declared at line 22 — use it.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace execSync call with await execFileAsync('npx', ['tsx', 'scripts/sync-audit-findings.ts', indexPath], { stdio: 'inherit', cwd: resolveRepoPath('IFleet') })

findings.title: handleMessageCreate: controlPlane.postCommand has no try/catch — user gets no feedback on failure
findings.detail: src/discord/handlers/message-create.ts line 55: await controlPlane.postCommand(...) is not wrapped in try/catch. If the control plane is down, the error propagates to client.ts's top-level handler which logs to console but sends no reply to the Discord user. The user's message silently disappears.
findings.fix_sketch: Wrap postCommand in try/catch and reply with an ephemeral error message on failure

findings.title: void shutdown().then(...) discards rejection — process hangs on teardown error
findings.detail: src/discord/index.ts line 60: void shutdown().then(() => process.exit(0)) — if shutdown() rejects, the rejection is silently discarded (void), process.exit(0) never fires, and the process hangs until SIGKILL. PM2 will eventually force-kill it, but the hang window wastes resources.
findings.fix_sketch: Add .catch(err => { console.error('[discord] shutdown error:', err); process.exit(1); })

findings.title: formatAutoBanditDecidedBy: both branches return identical output — dead if-branch
findings.detail: src/agents/proposer/auto-approve.ts lines 74-80: the if (Number.isInteger(threshold)) branch and the fallthrough both return `auto-bandit-${threshold}`. The comment says integers should strip a trailing .0, but Number.isInteger already guarantees no fractional part in the template literal — the two branches are identical. The dead if obscures intent.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the if block entirely and keep just: return `auto-bandit-${threshold}`;

findings.title: TERMINAL_SPRINT_KINDS includes 'aborted' which is not a valid SprintState kind
findings.detail: dashboard/server.ts line 23: TERMINAL_SPRINT_KINDS = new Set(['failed', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'aborted']). The SprintState union only has: queued | planning | running | paused | cancelled | completed | failed. 'aborted' is not a valid kind — it can never appear and is dead set membership.

findings.title: Trailing whitespace in defaultWarn and defaultTelemetry function bodies
findings.detail: src/agents/proposer/scorer.ts lines 192 and 197: both defaultWarn and defaultTelemetry have a line of trailing whitespace (a single space) as their first statement before the console call. Cosmetic but will trigger lint on whitespace-sensitive configs.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the trailing-whitespace-only lines inside both function bodies
